extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3 (priority 50), whose condition is the conjunction (prior_gi_bleed AND heart_failure AND asthma AND low_ejection_fraction AND g6pd_deficiency). rec_drug_b is set true only by r5 (priority 50), whose condition is prior_gi_bleed alone. r3's antecedent strictly implies prior_gi_bleed=true, so any input firing r3 necessarily also fires r5. No rule sets either output to false; defaults apply only when an output is left unset, and priority/order cannot suppress either rule since each is the sole rule for its own output. Setting prior_gi_bleed, heart_failure, asthma, low_ejection_fraction, and g6pd_deficiency all true (other inputs false/irrelevant) makes r3 fire (rec_drug_a=true) and r5 fire (rec_drug_b=true), so rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b are jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property. The bradycardia/immunosuppressed/anticoagulated rules only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant to this property.
